scope.title: WD-40 FY2025 synthetic monthly P&L reporting semantics
scope.purpose: Define the reviewed business meaning and one conditionally valid Reporting Engine use for the frozen prepared monthly P&L fixture.
included_subjects: prepared statement values at statement-line, fiscal-month, and scenario grain
the reviewed statement row and period structure

caveats: The notes govern this frozen synthetic fixture only.
The profile does not prove business meaning.
The snapshot fingerprint must not replace the exact prepared-file digest.
The recipe transports source-key values and does not authorize renderer-side arithmetic.

evidence.claim: The prepared table is a synthetic monthly statement fixture at one row_key by period by scenario grain and is not an issuer actual monthly disclosure.
notes: The public controls support quarter and fiscal-year tie-outs, not the synthetic monthly phasing.

evidence.claim: The origin profile contains 168 rows across the nine expected columns, including 14 row keys, 12 monthly periods, and one SYN scenario.
The reviewed recipe maps period, value, row key, and scenario columns and defines 14 source-key statement rows over 12 SYN periods without renderer formulas.
notes: Statement structure is package-owned rather than inferred from column names.

metrics.label: Prepared statement value
metrics.definition: The signed prepared P&L amount in USD thousands for exactly one statement row, fiscal month, and scenario.
metrics.rationale: The fixture notes and reviewed mapping establish a signed statement amount, while additive use is safe only across periods for the same statement line and scenario; subtotals and detail lines must never be summed together.
caveats: Rows include both detail lines and calculated statement subtotals, so cross-line aggregation double counts.

metrics.label: Display order
metrics.definition: An ordinal row-layout field copied from the reviewed statement structure; it is not a business measure.
metrics.rationale: The recipe owns statement ordering; treating display_order as an analyzable metric would create meaningless calculations.
caveats: May be used mechanically for layout validation only.

inference_reasons: numeric column without a strong metric-class hint

dimensions.label: Line type
dimensions.definition: A layout annotation identifying detail, subtotal, or total rows.
dimensions.rationale: The selected analysis uses the reviewed recipe for statement structure; grouping values by line_type would aggregate non-independent statement rows.
caveats: May be used mechanically for layout checks, but not as an analytical grouping.

dimensions.label: Statement line item
dimensions.definition: The stable key for one prepared P&L statement row defined by the reviewed statement recipe.
dimensions.rationale: The fixture and recipe use each row_key as the source-key identity for exactly one ordered statement row.
caveats: The keys include detail, subtotal, and total rows and do not form additive peers.

dimensions.label: Statement scenario
dimensions.definition: The explicit statement scenario; the only reviewed value in this snapshot is SYN, meaning synthetic monthly phasing.
dimensions.rationale: The fixture notes define the monthly values as synthetic and every origin-snapshot row carries the explicit SYN marker.
caveats: SYN must not be relabeled as actual, plan, forecast, or issuer disclosure.

dimensions.label: Unit column
dimensions.definition: A constant row-level repetition of the dataset-wide USD-thousands unit.
dimensions.rationale: Unit is constant in the reviewed snapshot and belongs to metric metadata, not to analytical grouping.
caveats: A snapshot with mixed units would require a semantic-layer revision.

periods.label: Fiscal month
periods.definition: The YYYY-MM label for one synthetic fiscal month in WD-40 FY2025, from September 2024 through August 2025.
periods.rationale: The prepared table and reviewed recipe contain the same complete ordered set of 12 fiscal-month labels.
caveats: The month labels are real calendar periods, but the monthly phasing is synthetic.

periods.label: Period start
periods.definition: A mechanical first-calendar-day boundary redundant with period.period.
periods.rationale: The selected statement capability requires one period axis; period.period is the reviewed display and key field, so this redundant boundary is excluded.
caveats: May be used for mechanical reconciliation, not as a second reporting period role.

periods.label: Period end
periods.definition: A mechanical last-calendar-day boundary redundant with period.period.
periods.rationale: The selected statement capability requires one period axis; period.period is the reviewed display and key field, so this redundant boundary is excluded.
caveats: May be used for mechanical reconciliation, not as a second reporting period role.

period_rules.label: All twelve prepared FY2025 months
period_rules.rationale: The reviewed reporting use is the complete 12-month statement table and the recipe explicitly orders every available month.
caveats: A bounded subset requires an explicit reviewed handoff revision.

analysis_policies.label: Prepared monthly P&L statement table
analysis_policies.question_family: Show the exact prepared synthetic monthly P&L values in their reviewed statement structure.
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Transport the reviewed prepared values into a deterministic statement table with complete sealed cell closure.
conditions: Use only the exact prepared snapshot independently bound by file SHA-256 in the reporting handoff request.
Require all 12 recipe periods in order and scenario SYN only.
Render the source values at row_key by period by scenario grain without renderer-side formulas or cross-line aggregation.
Use capability statement.pnl_table for the already selected structured_statement_values analysis; this policy does not perform chart selection.
Preserve the synthetic-fixture label and withhold issuer-actual or publication claims.
analysis_policies.rationale: The reviewed semantic roles exactly satisfy the statement.pnl_table contract, while the conditions prevent the mechanically additive value profile from authorizing unsafe cross-line arithmetic or misrepresenting synthetic data.
caveats: This is a bounded reporting transport policy, not due-diligence interpretation.
The semantic layer is model-reviewed and has not received human accounting review.

notes: Review applies only to this frozen synthetic preparation fixture and the one conditional analysis policy.
This semantic layer is explicitly not human-reviewed.
Deterministic validation proves contract wiring and snapshot compatibility, not the truth of semantic judgment.

boundaries.deterministic_validation_scope: Schema, identifiers, evidence references, profile bindings, exact manifest intent joins, required role coverage, and snapshot compatibility only.
